failure_surface: Agent received AttributeError when writing to merged cells: 'MergedCell' object attribute 'value' is read-only. Code initially attempted direct cell assignment without handling merge state.
root_cause: The agent did not check for merged cells in the target range before attempting to write formulas. In openpyxl, accessing cells within a merged range returns a MergedCell object where only the top-left cell is writable. Writing to other cells in the range causes AttributeError.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing to a target cell range, detect if cells are merged using ws.merged_cells.ranges, unmerge the range if necessary, then apply formulas to each individual cell.
evidence: Initial execution failed with: "AttributeError: 'MergedCell' object attribute 'value' is read-only" at line 21 (ws[cell_ref].value = formula)
Diagnostic step revealed: '--- All Merged Ranges --- ... J23:N23'
Agent corrected by adding Step 1: 'Unmerge the J23:N23 range if it exists' before applying formulas
Final execution succeeded only after unmerge operation was added

memory_items.title: Check Merged Cells Before Writing
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect ws.merged_cells.ranges before writing to a cell range to identify merged cells that may block assignment.
memory_items.generalization: In spreadsheet APIs like openpyxl, merged cells restrict write operations. Detect and unmerge before modifying affected cells.

memory_items.title: Formulas Must Be Applied Per Cell
memory_items.lesson: When targeting a multi-cell range, assign the formula to each cell individually; formulas do not auto-fill across cells.
memory_items.generalization: For operations on cell ranges (J23:N23), iterate over each cell in the target range and explicitly set its value.

memory_items.title: Validate Cell State During Execution
memory_items.lesson: Use diagnostic prints or conditional checks to verify cell properties (merged status, data type) before critical write operations.
memory_items.generalization: Add pre-flight validation steps to catch structural constraints (merges, hidden rows, protected cells) before modifications.

edits.content:
## 6. Merged Cell Handling
- **Detection**: Always check `ws.merged_cells.ranges` before writing to any cell range to identify merged cells.
- **Pre-write Preparation**: If target cells are part of a merged range, unmerge using `unmerge_cells()` before assignment, since merged cells restrict write operations to the top-left cell only.
- **Range Iteration**: When applying formulas or values to a multi-cell range (e.g., J23:N23), iterate over each individual cell and set values explicitly—formulas do not auto-fill across cells in openpyxl.
- **Validation**: After modifications, verify that merged ranges no longer affect the target area and that all expected cells contain the intended formula or value.
